skill.name: ui-component-patterns
skill.description: Guides creation of reusable React and TypeScript UI components using props API design, composition, custom hooks, and performance patterns.
skill.summary: Design reusable React components with clear props, composition patterns, hooks, and practical structure.
skill.author: supercent-io

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns. AI review found these are documentation false positives inside Markdown code fences, reference links, tags, and related-skill links, with no executable scripts or prompt-injection attempts found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fences Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Backticks appear in TSX and plain Markdown examples for React component patterns. They are documentation delimiters and template strings, not Ruby shell execution or runnable install commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown fences, TSX examples, or inline tags. No executable Ruby code, shell command instruction, or command invocation path is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reference URLs Flagged as Network Activity
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs are documentation references to React pattern and UI library resources. The skill does not instruct the agent to fetch, post, or transmit data to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Each URL is in the References section as a Markdown link. No credential handling, telemetry, or network request code was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Relative Documentation Links Flagged as Path Traversal
low_findings.description: The ../ paths are relative Markdown links to related skill documents. They are not file read/write operations and do not request traversal of user files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The paths appear only in a Related Skills list. There is no filesystem API, command, or instruction to open arbitrary paths.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Tags and Text Flagged as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: Weak-crypto alerts match descriptive text, metadata, headings, examples, and tag lines. No hash algorithm, encryption routine, or password storage guidance is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching locations contain UI component descriptions, tags, or abbreviated metadata. No cryptographic implementation or security-sensitive algorithm choice was found.

content.value_statement: Teams need consistent UI components that stay easy to reuse and maintain. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical React patterns for scalable component design.
actual_capabilities: Designs clear React props APIs with TypeScript interfaces and defaults.
Explains compound component and composition patterns for reusable UI.
Shows render prop patterns for flexible item rendering and selection.
Separates component state and behavior into custom hooks.
Recommends component file structures with tests, stories, styles, and exports.
Highlights memoization patterns that reduce avoidable React re-renders.
limitations: Focuses on React and TypeScript patterns, not every frontend framework.
Provides guidance and examples, not a complete production component library.
Does not replace accessibility testing with real assistive technologies.
Needs project context to match existing design system conventions.

use_cases.title: Create a Shared Component Library
use_cases.description: Define reusable Button, Card, Modal, and Dropdown patterns with consistent props and file organization.

use_cases.title: Standardize Design System Implementation
use_cases.description: Turn design tokens and interaction rules into predictable React components with documented composition patterns.

use_cases.title: Refactor Repeated UI Code
use_cases.description: Extract duplicated screens into reusable components, hooks, and compound patterns without hiding important behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Basic Component API
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one reusable component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a reusable React Button component API for my app. Include TypeScript props, defaults, variants, and accessibility notes.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor Existing UI Into Components
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request using current code or screenshots.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this repeated UI pattern and propose reusable React components. Explain props, composition boundaries, and file structure.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Component Family
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a React component family for cards, headers, bodies, and footers. Use compound components and clear TypeScript contracts.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Component Architecture
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a component library.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this component architecture for prop complexity, composition, accessibility, performance, and maintainability. Recommend prioritized changes.

output_examples.input: Create a reusable Button component for three variants and loading state.
output_examples.output: A props API with variant, size, disabled, loading, event handler, default button type, and accessibility guidance.

output_examples.input: Refactor a product card with header, image, content, and actions.
output_examples.output: A compound Card structure with separate header, body, and footer pieces plus usage guidance for product screens.

output_examples.input: Improve a dropdown that renders different item layouts.
output_examples.output: A render prop design that accepts typed items, custom rendering, selection callbacks, and predictable open state behavior.

best_practices: Start with the smallest stable props API before adding variants or escape hatches.
Use composition for layout flexibility and custom hooks for shared behavior.
Document expected usage with tests, stories, and accessibility states.
anti_patterns: Do not add many unrelated props to make one component solve every case.
Do not place API calls or complex business rules inside presentational components.
Do not optimize with memoization before measuring real render problems.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill focus on?
faq.answer: It focuses on React and TypeScript component design patterns.

faq.question: Can it help build a design system?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports component APIs, composition, file structure, and reusable implementation patterns.

faq.question: Does it generate production-ready components?
faq.answer: It provides strong patterns and examples. You still need project-specific styling, tests, and accessibility checks.

faq.question: Can it review existing components?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the component code and ask for an architecture, props, accessibility, or performance review.

faq.question: Does it cover accessibility?
faq.answer: It includes accessibility reminders for roles, aria attributes, keyboard behavior, and component state.

faq.question: Is this useful for Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The prompts and guidance work for Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows.
